title: trisetum carpaticum
intro: Trisetum carpaticum, also known as Carpathian hair-grass, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestion, although more scientific research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, Trisetum carpaticum is often used as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems are dried or made into infusions for medicinal purposes.
